CogAgentLab
Architecture Deck Current system Evidence

Architecture note · historical terminology

“Conscious” was a role name, not a sentience claim.

The March 2025 runtime called one routed component Conscious_Agent. Its observable job was narrower: after a new percept, it wrote a revisable account of task state, progress, and uncertainty for the other components to use. This page explains the theory behind that design, what the implementation supports, and why CogAgentLab now calls the role Belief_State_Agent.

Start with the qualification Return to the architecture

The claim boundary

Nothing in the run establishes subjective experience, self-awareness, phenomenal consciousness, or a persistent inner self.

“Functional consciousness” was historical shorthand for globally available task state and coordinated cognitive control. The public explanation uses operational terms because they are more precise.

The shortest correct reading

Continuity was maintained, not possessed.

The system did not contain a single agent with a continuous stream of awareness. It maintained continuity as an engineering invariant: preserve a task-relevant state, expose it to the right operator, require action to return through environment feedback, and recover information when the active context became incomplete.

Operational thesis Coherent long-horizon behavior can emerge when state, control, feedback, and memory are made explicit—even though each individual model call is temporary and fallible.
Mechanism Terminology Workspace theory Memory boundary Evolution Evidence boundary

Mechanistic account

Four operations turned separate model calls into one task loop.

The architecture treated continuity as a control problem. Specialized roles could contribute only through a constrained transition graph, and every action had to encounter the environment before the central state could be revised.

  1. 01

    Externalize state

    A textual belief state compressed the latest percept, task progress, unresolved facts, and uncertainty into a representation later roles could inspect.

  2. 02

    Constrain control

    The AutoGen GroupChatManager allowed only legal next speakers. The route—not an unconstrained conversation—determined whether the system planned, acted, focused, retrieved, imagined, or learned.

  3. 03

    Close the loop

    Planning produced motor output; the environment executed it; external perception returned the result; only then did the belief state update. This cycle repeatedly forced reasoning back onto observable evidence.

  4. 04

    Restore context

    Focus replayed the task and latest percept. Memory retrieval restored the current-game observation history and representative learned rules when those details were no longer salient in the shared context.

Historical names, observable functions

What Conscious_Agent actually did.

The name came from the project’s cognitive analogy. The trace-level role was an epistemic state updater: it interpreted a percept and emitted a textual hypothesis labeled World Model:. It did not execute actions, and its output was neither guaranteed true nor executable as a simulator.

Historical term Observed or intended function Reader-facing term
Conscious_Agent Revises the shared account of the current task after perception. Belief-state updater
World Model: A fallible textual hypothesis about state, progress, and uncertainty. Belief state
Global workspace Shared group-chat context plus a constrained next-speaker policy. Shared state and routing
Autopilot loop The short belief → plan → act → perceive cycle used for ordinary progress. Grounded action loop
Focus loop Replays the task and latest valid observation without taking an action. Context recovery

Why the qualification matters

In philosophy and neuroscience, consciousness can refer to subjective experience, reportability, access, self-modeling, wakefulness, or other distinct phenomena. The evaluated artifacts address none of those as a scientific measurement. They show a software system coordinating specialized operations through shared state. Using “belief-state updater” names the implemented responsibility without importing a stronger claim.

Why invoke Global Workspace Theory?

The analogy concerns access and coordination.

Global Workspace Theory motivated a division between specialized processors and information made broadly available for control. In the runtime, selected role outputs entered one bounded conversation history; later roles could use that history, while the transition graph limited which role could speak next.

The analogy helps explain

  • why specialized perception, planning, action, and memory roles share state;
  • why only selected outputs influence the next operation;
  • why the system needs routing, re-grounding, and memory retrieval; and
  • how a temporary model call can participate in a longer-lived task process.

The analogy does not establish

  • subjective or phenomenal experience;
  • a unified self persisting independently of stored state;
  • biological fidelity to human cognition; or
  • that global availability is sufficient for consciousness.

The unpublished draft used functional consciousness in this narrow access-and-control sense and explicitly disclaimed sentience and human-like subjective experience. That framing is useful intellectual history, not an empirical conclusion of the 139-game evaluation.

Theory versus evaluated configuration

Episodex was broader than the memory used in the canonical run.

The project’s memory theory organized experience into evolving belief states, episode traces, and reusable conceptual abstractions. The March 2025 run implemented only part of that intended system, so theory and evaluation must remain separate.

Active in the run

Current-game trace

The system appended time-ordered observations and could retrieve that local history during the same game. This supported within-task continuity.

Active across games

Conceptual rules

Learned rules accumulated, were embedded with MiniLM, clustered with KMeans, and reduced to representative rules for later retrieval.

Not active in the run

Prior-episode transcripts

Earlier game transcripts or earlier belief histories were not retrieved in the canonical evaluation, despite appearing in the broader Episodex design.

Dynamic episode segmentation, relevance-driven prior-episode retrieval, and broader claims about memory-driven generalization belong to the design program. The 87.05% outcome should not be read as a clean causal test of every proposed memory mechanism.

Terminology and architecture evolution

The engineering thesis survived the vocabulary.

  1. March 2025

    Evaluated runtime

    Conscious_Agent produced World Model: updates inside a constrained AutoGen transition graph.

  2. Post-implementation draft

    Functional framing

    The unpublished paper described global access, memory coordination, grounding, and goal-directed control as “functional consciousness,” while disclaiming sentience.

  3. Summer 2025

    Summit presentation

    The deck presented the broader cognitive and memory architecture. Its diagrams are historical design artifacts, not an exact manifest of the evaluated configuration.

  4. Current CogAgentLab

    Operational names and typed state

    Belief_State_Agent, typed DecisionState, guarded action bursts, and evidence-triggered deliberation make the same control questions explicit without the consciousness label.

Epistemic status

What is evidence, what is interpretation, and what is not claimed.

Source-audited

Runtime structure

Role names, allowed transitions, tool paths, active memory channels, action budgets, and trace frequencies come from the same-day source and preserved run artifacts.

Interpretation

Continuity theory

The account of state, access, control, and memory explains why the design may cohere. It is an architectural interpretation, not a causal decomposition of the 87.05% result.

Not claimed

Conscious experience

No artifact tests sentience, subjective experience, selfhood, or biological similarity. The page makes no claim about them.

Sources and further inspection

  • Evaluated runtime reconstruction — primary source for the March 2025 configuration.
  • Machine-readable interpretation record — aliases, mechanisms, sources, and boundaries.
  • Summit deck manifest — provenance and integrity for the historical presentation.
  • Current architecture manifest — source audit of CogAgentLab’s present control structure.
  • Claim-to-evidence ledger — outcome and attribution evidence for the wider showcase.

The unpublished paper draft by Yixiao Wang and Aaron Zheng was supplied by Eduardo Cortes and used only as an interpretive source for the historical theory. It is not redistributed and does not establish the evaluation total, dataset split, or exact runtime configuration.
